explanation:
**Nutrition and fitness** are based on physical principles (energy balance) and biological factors (macronutrients, micronutrients). This calculator uses formulas validated by the scientific community (ACSM, ISSN, NIH).

## How It's Calculated

Calories burned during a boxing session are calculated using the METs (Metabolic Equivalent of Task) formula, multiplied by your body weight and session duration.

The calculator applies the formula with your input values, checking typical ranges and alerting you to values outside reasonable bounds.

## Standard Macronutrient Distribution

| Macronutrient | % of Calories | Calories per Gram |
|---|---|---|
| Protein | 15–35% | 4 kcal/g |
| Carbohydrates | 45–65% | 4 kcal/g |
| Fat | 20–35% | 9 kcal/g |
| Alcohol | — | 7 kcal/g |
| Fiber | Minimum 25–38g/day | — |

## When to Consult a Nutritionist

- If you have medical conditions (diabetes, hypertension, kidney disease) requiring personalized nutrition.
- If you want to build significant muscle mass or compete in a sport.
- If you struggle with disordered eating patterns (binging, extreme restriction).
- If you've made no progress toward your goal after 3+ months.
- To plan complete vegan or vegetarian nutrition.

## Final Notes

This calculation is an informational estimate. For critical decisions (medical, financial, legal), consult a qualified professional. Values are current to 2026 and reviewed regularly.

faq.q: How many calories do you burn boxing?
faq.a: It depends on your weight, session intensity, and duration. A 75 kg person burns approximately 500–800 calories in a one-hour boxing session. Use this calculator for your specific stats.
MET measures exercise intensity. One MET equals your resting metabolic rate. Shadow boxing = 6 MET, heavy bag = 9 MET, sparring = 12 MET, kickboxing = 10 MET.

faq.q: Does shadow boxing burn fewer calories than heavy bag work?
faq.a: Yes. Shadow boxing (MET 6) is moderate intensity, while heavy bag (MET 9) is high intensity. Sparring (MET 12) burns the most calories due to its vigorous, full-contact nature.

faq.q: How many calories do you burn sparring for 45 minutes?
faq.a: A 75 kg person burns approximately 560 calories in 45 minutes of sparring at MET 12. Adjust the calculation for your weight and duration.

faq.q: Does kickboxing burn more calories than boxing?
faq.a: Kickboxing (MET 10) burns more calories than shadow boxing (MET 6) or light boxing, but slightly less than sparring (MET 12).

faq.q: How does boxing compare to running for calorie burn?
faq.a: Boxing is similar to running at a 10-minute mile pace in terms of calorie burn. Both are excellent full-body cardio workouts.

faq.q: How many calories should I burn per day?
faq.a: Daily calorie needs vary by age, sex, weight, and activity level. Average adult women: 1,800–2,200 kcal; adult men: 2,200–3,000 kcal. Use a TDEE calculator for personalized estimates.

faq.q: Does weight affect how many calories you burn boxing?
faq.a: Yes, significantly. Heavier individuals burn more calories doing the same activity. The formula uses weight as a multiplier: more weight = more calories burned.

faq.q: Is this calculator accurate for beginners?
faq.a: This provides a reasonable estimate based on scientific formulas. Individual results vary based on fitness level, technique, and intensity. Use it as a starting point.

sources.name: NIST — National Institute of Standards and Technology

dataUpdate.notes: Fixed formula, no external data update required
